name: @radzor/rate-limiter
description: In-memory rate limiting with token bucket and sliding window algorithms. Zero dependencies, automatic cleanup, and HTTP header generation.

inputs.name: algorithm
inputs.description: Rate limiting algorithm. Token bucket allows bursts; sliding window is stricter.

inputs.name: maxRequests
inputs.description: Maximum number of requests allowed per window.

inputs.name: keyPrefix
inputs.description: Optional prefix for rate limit keys (useful to separate different endpoints).

outputs.name: rateLimitResult
outputs.description: Result including allowed, remaining, resetAt, and retryAfterMs.

events.name: onAllowed
events.description: Fired when a request is allowed through.

events.name: onBlocked
events.description: Fired when a request is rate-limited.

actions.name: consume

params.name: key
params.description: Unique identifier for the client (e.g. IP address, user ID, API key).

actions.description: Consume a token and return whether the request is allowed.

actions.name: check
actions.description: Check rate limit status without consuming a token.

actions.name: reset
actions.description: Reset the rate limit for a specific key.

actions.name: getHeaders

params.name: result
params.description: A rate limit result to generate headers from.

actions.description: Generate standard HTTP rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-*, Retry-After).

actions.name: destroy
actions.description: Stop cleanup interval and clear all stored data.

llm.constraints: In-memory only — rate limits reset on process restart. For distributed systems, use an external store (Redis). Automatic cleanup prevents memory leaks.
